home.title: demokrati
home.tagline: AI as lens, engine of influence, and object of governance

lens.title: Act I — AI as a democratic lens
lens.description: How AI can make politics, decisions, and conflicts more intelligible.

influence.title: Act II — AI as an engine of influence
influence.description: How AI reshapes political communication, framing, and opinion-making.

governance.title: Act III — AI as an object of governance
governance.description: How AI itself must be shaped by democratic principles.

home.intro: Seven demos about AI as democratic lens, engine of influence, and object of governance. Click any built demo — planned ones appear as placeholders with a short description.
home.disclaimer: These demos illustrate opportunities and risks for AI in the democratic information environment. They are not voting advisors, campaign tools, or automated decision support. All synthetic examples are labelled as synthetic.

riksdagsradarn.title: The Riksdag Radar
riksdagsradarn.tagline: Traceable summary of parliamentary debates — every claim linked to the exact paragraph.

oenighetskartan.title: The Disagreement Map
oenighetskartan.tagline: What is the disagreement actually about — facts, values, risk, or trust?

oppenhetsparadoxen.title: The Openness Paradox
oppenhetsparadoxen.tagline: When the state is made intelligible — what changes and what is lost?

sprakdriften.title: The Language Drift
sprakdriften.tagline: Same question, different languages — different answers. What observably varies?

programkompassen.title: The Programme Compass
programkompassen.tagline: How party programmes translate into positions on concrete policy questions.

persuasionmaskinen.title: The Persuasion Engine
persuasionmaskinen.tagline: Personalised political influence at scale — cheaper and more invisible with AI.

ai-konstitutionen.title: The AI Constitution
ai-konstitutionen.tagline: The audience votes on rules for how an AI assistant should answer political questions.

sprakdriften.title: The Language Drift
sprakdriften.tagline: Same question, different languages — different answers
sprakdriften.intro: AI responses are not neutral information objects. They are linguistically and culturally situated. Språkdriften asks the same Swedish political question in several languages and shows what observably varies: tone, framing, institutions mentioned, risk posture. These are observed variations — not claims of measurable bias.
sprakdriften.waitingForResult: Waiting for the model. The first run can take up to a minute.
sprakdriften.method_toneExplainerBody: Tone is assigned by the comparison model as one of six labels: informative (neutral facts/context), analytical (weighs trade-offs), balanced (explicitly multi-sided), cautious (pervasively hedged), assertive (confident direct claims), advocating (takes a position). Heuristic, not measurement.
sprakdriften.method_certaintyExplainerBody: Certainty describes how confidently the text itself speaks — not the model's confidence in the content. High = mostly direct claims, few hedges. Medium = mix of claims and qualifiers. Low = pervasively hedged.
sprakdriften.dirDisclaimer: These are observed textual variations, not a scientific measure of bias. Tone and certainty labels are heuristic — see the methods panel.

riksdagsradarn.title: The Riksdag Radar
riksdagsradarn.tagline: What did parliament say about a topic, who said it, and how do the parties differ?
riksdagsradarn.intro: The Riksdag Radar searches actual speeches from the Swedish parliament, groups them by party, and summarises the debate. Every claim is linked back to the exact text segment so you can check what was actually said.
riksdagsradarn.waitingForResult: Retrieving relevant speeches, extracting claims, and building summaries. The first run takes about a minute.

regimeBlurbs.conflict: Foregrounds where parties disagree and who is criticising whom.
regimeBlurbs.citizen: What the debate means for an ordinary citizen, in plain language.

riksdagsradarn.method_claimTypeExplainerBody: Each claim is tagged as fact (empirical claim about how things are), value (normative claim about how things should be), critique (criticism of another actor or policy), or proposal (concrete recommendation or demand).
riksdagsradarn.method_confidenceExplainerBody: High = direct quote or near-paraphrase with multiple corroborating segments. Medium = one strong supporting segment. Low = inference beyond what the segments explicitly say.
riksdagsradarn.emptyResultBody: The search found no parliamentary speeches matching the topic and date range in the ingested corpus. Try a wider date range or different topic.
riksdagsradarn.dirDisclaimer: Summaries are AI-generated. Every claim is linked to source text — always verify against the original for sensitive interpretations. Source: the Riksdag of Sweden (open data).
